zh.lifeSummary: 當Sr Edward Coleman於1486年出生在英國素福爾可格亞特 瓦爾迪費爾德時，他的父親Sir John Edward Coleman30歲，母親Katherine Fulsnape26歲。他於大約1515年與Dame Katherine Fulsnape在英國素福爾可結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1578年8月26日在他的家鄉享年92歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可蕾托 瓦爾迪費爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sr Edward Coleman于1486年出生在英国素福爾可格亞特 瓦爾迪費爾德时，他的父亲Sir John Edward Coleman30岁，母亲Katherine Fulsnape26岁。他于大约1515年与Dame Katherine Fulsnape在英国素福爾可结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1578年8月26日在他的家乡享年92岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可蕾托 瓦爾迪費爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Coleman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

variants.name: Kalman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Bela, Gabor, Sandor, Imre, Lajos, Geza, Gyula, Katalin, Zoltan.

Hungarian (Kálmán): from the old Hungarian personal name Kálmán, meaning ‘remainder’ (from Turkic kal ‘to remain’), hence a protective name, which was given to infants to ward off evil and harmful spirits. In medieval times the name became particularly popular because it was confused with the German Christian name Koloman of Irish origin (see Kolman 2).

Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish personal name Kalmen, an everyday form of Kloynemes (from Hebrew Kalonimos, which is from Greek kalos ‘lovely’ or kallos ‘beauty’ + onyma ‘name’). This Hebrew name is first recorded in the Talmud and has been used continuously since then. Among Hungarian Jews, it was sometimes confused with 1 above.

variants.name: Colman
meanings: Irish (Mayo and Sligo) and English: variant of   Coleman  .
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kohlmann   or   Kollmann  .
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

variants.name: Clifford
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Collman
meanings: English (Middlesex and Surrey): variant of   Coleman  .
Americanized form of German   Kollmann  , or of   Kohlmann  .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: When Sr Edward Coleman was born in 1486, in Great Waldingfield, Suffolk, England, his father, Sir John Edward Coleman, was 30 and his mother, Katherine Fulsnape, was 26. He married Dame Katherine Fulsnape about 1515, in Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 26 August 1578, in his hometown, at the age of 92, and was buried in Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sr Edward Coleman est né en 1486, à Great Waldingfield, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Edward Coleman, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Katherine Fulsnape, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Dame Katherine Fulsnape environ 1515, à Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 26 août 1578, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 92 ans, et a été enterré à Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sr Edward Coleman 1486 in Great Waldingfield, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Edward Coleman 30 und seine Mutter Katherine Fulsnape 26. Er hat um 1515 in Suffolk, England Dame Katherine Fulsnape geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 26. it.lifeSummary: Quando Sr Edward Coleman è nato il nel 1486 nel Great Waldingfield, Suffolk, England, il suo padre, Sir John Edward Coleman, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Katherine Fulsnape, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Dame Katherine Fulsnape circa 1515, nel Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 26 agosto 1578, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 92 anni, e fu sepolto a Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sr Edward Colemanが1486年にイギリススフォルクグレアト ワルディングフィールドで生まれた時，父Sir John Edward Colemanは30歳，母Katherine Fulsnapeは26歳であった。約1515年にイギリススフォルクでDame Katherine Fulsnapeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1578年8月26日に故郷で92歳で死去し，イギリススフォルクリットル ワルディングフィールドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sr Edward Coleman(이)가 1486년 영국 수폴크 그리트 월딩필드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Edward Coleman(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Katherine Fulsnape(은)는 26세였다. 그는 대략 1515년 영국 수폴크에서 Dame Katherine Fulsnape(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1578년 8월 26일 그의 고향에서 92세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 리틀 월딩필드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sr Edward Coleman nasceu em 1486, em Great Waldingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Edward Coleman, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Katherine Fulsnape, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Dame Katherine Fulsnape aproximadamente 1515, em Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 26 de agosto de 1578, em sua cidade natal, com 92 anos, e foi sepultado em Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sr Edward Coleman родился в 1486 г. в местности грит валдингфиелд, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John Edward Coleman было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Katherine Fulsnape, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Dame Katherine Fulsnape около 1515 г. в местности суффолк, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 26 августа 1578 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 92 лет и был похоронен в местности литл валдингфиелд, суффолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sr Edward Coleman nació en 1486, en Great Waldingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Edward Coleman, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Katherine Fulsnape, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Dame Katherine Fulsnape aproximadamente 1515, en Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 26 de agosto de 1578, en su pueblo, a la edad de 92 años, y fue sepultado en Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra.
